mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 9.0 percent, it signals a structural shift from recessionary labor market slack to expansionary hiring, which improves consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.0 percent, or the signal's predictive power decays to zero within 6 months of the threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate synchronized real-economy expansion across manufacturing and residential sectors. This joint signal compresses risk premiums and sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with stagnant GDP growth creates stagflationary expectations that force rapid equity repricing. This mechanism generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon when inflation accelerates faster than output.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between the inflation-output gap and 5-day returns remains below 0.15 over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly proxies for corporate earnings momentum. Positive GDP acceleration consistently drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions cascade through equity valuations.
failure_condition: The signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over any 5-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between positive and negative GDP growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls increase by more than 200,000 in a single month, it confirms robust labor demand that historically precedes sustained equity rallies. This threshold breach predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following payroll spikes are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances alongside accelerating inflation signal overheating demand that anticipates aggressive Federal Reserve tightening. This interaction compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts negative returns in fewer than 45% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in industrial production following a contraction phase signals supply-side normalization and improved corporate capacity utilization. This supply recovery drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as margin expectations improve.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances following INDPRO rebounds, or the signal's rank correlation with 5-day returns remains below 0.05 over a 2-year test window.
